reasoning: Historically, reaching 18 consecutive weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 is exceptionally rare, with no album achieving this feat since the 1990s (MC Hammer's 'Please Don't Hurt 'Em' with 18 weeks). While Taylor Swift's 'The Life of a Showgirl' had a massive 4 million-unit debut and has held the top spot for 5 weeks, its weekly units have already declined sharply to 120k by week 5. To reach 18 consecutive weeks, it would have to maintain the #1 spot through mid-February 2026. This requires surviving the entire holiday season, a period when legacy Christmas albums (e.g., Michael Bublé, Mariah Carey, Nat King Cole) frequently surge to the top of the chart thanks to massive streaming volume. Furthermore, Rolling Stone noted its shorter 12-track list makes sustaining high streaming figures difficult compared to her previous 31-track 'TTPD'. Given the steep unit drop-off, the impending holiday chart takeover, and potential high-profile Q4 releases like Drake's anticipated album, the likelihood of a continuous 18-week reign is close to zero.
